message:
docs(sqlmodel-core): clarify DumpOptions implementation status

Adds comprehensive documentation to DumpOptions fields explaining
which features are fully implemented vs API-compatible stubs:

Fully implemented:
- include/exclude: Field filtering works correctly
- exclude_none: Removes null values from output
- indent: JSON indentation formatting

API-compatible but NOT implemented (documented with NOTE comments):
- mode (Json vs Python): Both produce identical serde_json::Value output
  since Rust serialization is determined by Serialize implementations
- exclude_unset: Requires compile-time fields_set tracking infrastructure
- round_trip: Serialization fidelity handled by serde implementations

Also documents the difference between generic ModelDump trait
(for any Serialize type) vs SqlModelDump trait (requires Model metadata
for by_alias, exclude_defaults, exclude_computed_fields).

Adds nested model tests (Address, Person) to validate recursive
model serialization behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
